item.title: Lufthansa Reports Narrower Loss, Sees Risks from Fuel Supplies
item.description: Deutsche Lufthansa AG posted a smaller-than-expected loss in the first quarter as strong demand for longhaul flights helped offset volatile fuel costs and disruption from labor strikes.

item.title: Yen Rallies to Build on Gains Spurred by Recent Intervention
item.description: The yen rallied as much as 1.8% against the dollar, building upon gains triggered when Japanese officials intervened in markets to prop up their currency on April 30.

item.title: Sany Heavy Is Said to Consider IPO for Electric Unit in Hong Kong
item.description: Sany Heavy Industry Co. is considering an initial public offering for one of its units in Hong Kong, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Australian Pension Fund Tilts to US Over Local Stocks on AI Boom
item.description: Brighter Super, a A$37 billion ($26.8 billion) Australian pension fund, is tilting its portfolio away from the local share market in favor of global stocks as artificial intelligence continues to gain momentum in the US.

item.title: State Fund IPO Opens Door to Buy Into Uzbekistan’s Growth Story
item.description: Uzbekistan is kicking off a planned series of initial public offerings with the sale of about a 30% stake in the state investment fund, as part of the Central Asian nation’s push to entice international investors.

item.title: Soy Oil Falls from Three-Year High After Trump’s Hormuz Pause
item.description: Soybean oil contracts fell from their highest in three-and-a-half years, following a slump in crude after President Donald Trump said he would pause US-led efforts to guide ships through the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: New Zealand Eyes Fuel Storage Options in Singapore and Malaysia
item.description: New Zealand is looking at storing fuel in Malaysia and Singapore, a senior government minister said, as the impacts of the Iran war expose the risks from its limited domestic storage capacity.

item.title: Indian Small-Caps Rally 20% From March Lows to Near Bull Market
item.description: India’s small-cap stocks are staging a sharp comeback, underscoring a revival in risk appetite among investors after months of steep losses.

item.title: Momentum Is Trampling Quality in Market Records
item.description: It can be dangerous to stand in the way of a rally like this.

item.title: Poland Set to Match Steady Rates With Hawkish Tone on Iran Risks
item.description: Poland’s central bank is set to keep interest rates unchanged for a second month with policymakers expected to sharpen anti-inflation rhetoric as the conflict in Iran revives long-dormant price pressures.

item.title: Top Africa Ports Miss Refuel Gain Even as Iran War Diverts Ships
item.description: African ports are capturing only a fraction of shipping rerouted around the continent’s southern tip after the closure of the Strait of Hormuz, underscoring their limited ability to turn global trade disruptions into gains.

item.title: Oil Shock Drives Thailand Inflation to Near Top End of Target
item.description: Higher oil prices linked to the Iran conflict pushed Thailand’s inflation to near the top end of the central bank’s target, ending a yearlong stretch of falling prices.

item.title: Japan Strikes Combat Role in South China Sea in Post-War Pivot
item.description: Japan’s decision to send combat troops to the Philippines for the first time since World War II and participate in a ship-sinking drill in the South China Sea this week underscores Tokyo’s rising willingness to project military power on China’s doorstep.

item.title: Asian Currencies Rally on AI Enthusiasm, US-Iran Peace Hopes
item.description: Asian currencies extended a rebound as the artificial intelligence trade gathered pace and sentiment improved on hopes of a peace deal between the US and Iran.

item.title: China’s Tech Gauge Jumps to Record High on AI-Fueled Rally
item.description: A gauge of Chinese tech shares surged to a record high, joining a broader artificial intelligence-related rally sweeping across Asian markets as investors piled into growth sectors.

item.title: CATL Bears Retreat in Hong Kong After Blockbuster Share Sale
item.description: Traders are swiftly unwinding bearish positions against Contemporary Amperex Technology Co. Ltd.’s Hong Kong-listed shares as the focus shifts to the battery giant’s growth outlook following a $5 billion share sale.

item.title: Blue Owl Data Center Operator Stack Is Said to Consider $30 Billion Sale of Asia Operations
item.description: Stack Infrastructure Inc., a data center company owned by Blue Owl Capital, is considering options including a sale of its Asia operations, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: India’s Individual Investors Are Leaving Stock Picking to the Pros
item.description: Small investors own fewer stocks directly, pour money into the market via mutual funds.

item.title: Chinese Metals Demand Gets Lift as Mideast War Spurs Export Boom
item.description: Metal exports from China, especially aluminum, are getting a significant lift from the war in the Middle East, which has cut regional supplies while boosting demand for clean-tech products as fossil fuel prices soar.

item.title: Iron Ore Rises as China Reopening After Holiday Spurs Buying
item.description: Iron ore rose to the highest since October 2024 as China’s markets reopened after a five-day holiday.

item.title: China’s Surprise Services Upswing Shows Firms Absorb Price Shock
item.description: China’s services activity unexpectedly expanded at a stronger pace in April, a private survey showed, even as price shocks from the Iran war ripple through the economy and companies choose not to pass on higher costs to customers.

item.title: Coupang Warns of 2026 Slowdown After Data Breach Hits Spending
item.description: Coupang Inc. warned revenue growth will slow this year after a bigger-than-expected March-quarter loss, reflecting the extent to which a historic cyber-intrusion is depressing spending across South Korea’s biggest online retail platform.

item.title: US Unveiled Plan to Reopen Hormuz Hours Before Trump Shelved It
item.description: Top US officials spent Monday and Tuesday offering new details on the role the US military will play in helping ships pass through the Strait of Hormuz as part of President Donald Trump’s bid to break Iran’s chokehold over the waterway.

item.title: Sun Pharma Explores Funding Mix for $12 Billion Organon Deal
item.description: Global lenders to Sun Pharmaceutical Industries Ltd. are weighing multiple financing options for its proposed $12 billion acquisition of New York-listed healthcare company Organon & Co., according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Infratil Shares Surge as CDC Signs Monster Data Center Deal
item.description: CDC Data Centres is forecasting a surge in earnings over the next three years after agreeing Australia’s largest data center contract, stoking the value of its largest holder Infratil Ltd.

item.title: Samsung Hits $1 Trillion Valuation, Joining TSMC in Elite Club
item.description: Samsung Electronics Co. has reached a $1 trillion market valuation after booming demand for chips used in artificial intelligence saw the world’s largest memory maker’s stock more than quadruple over the past year.

item.title: Indonesia Tightens Rule on FX Purchases After Rupiah Hit New Record Low
item.description: Indonesia tightened rules on dollar purchases to defend the rupiah after it weakened to an all-time low.

item.title: Sydney Hedge Fund Regal Sees Inflows Jump for Inflation Hedges
item.description: Sydney-based fund manager Regal Partners Ltd. is on track to hit A$2 billion ($1.4 billion) in net inflows this year, as conflict in the Middle East spurs demand for investment strategies offering a hedge against inflation.

item.title: Private Credit Fund Touts Quick Profit Buying Debt at 65 Cents
item.description: A New Mountain Capital private credit fund that sold almost half-a-billion-dollars of assets at a discount earlier this year and used some of the cash to scoop up beaten-down loans says the strategy is already paying off.

item.title: South Korea’s Inflation Accelerates on Higher Oil Price Impact
item.description: South Korea’s consumer inflation picked up as surging energy costs began to feed more forcefully into the domestic economy, with price gains matching the fastest pace since July 2024.
